heading: Recommendation
body:
For your LDAPS (Secure Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) service to access Atlas clusters, MongoDB
recommends one of two configurations:

Using a VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) or VNet:

- Run your LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) server in a VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) or VNet.

- Establish a peering connection to your Atlas project.

- Use a public FQDN (fully qualified domain name) that resolves to the private IP address
  of your LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) server.

Using your data center:

- Run your LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) server with a public FQDN (fully qualified domain name) that resolves to a
  public IP address.

- Configure the LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) server to allow inbound access from the
  Atlas cluster nodes' public IP addresses.
